codeReview.summary: Implementation covers all 5 validation assertions. Football MatchCard upgraded with 5xl emoji flags in centered VS layout with gradient badge. BasketballCard enhanced with orange-to-red gradient background, decorative basketball emojis, court circle graphic, and gradient text price. ConcertCard redesigned with full-width artist image, dark gradient overlay (from-black/80 via-black/40 to-transparent) for text readability, and overlaid artist info. BannerCarousel test correctly updated to match gradient-based implementation. All category page banners (Football/Basketball/Concert) use sport-specific themed gradients with relevant icons. Design tokens (shadow-card, shadow-elevated, rounded-xl) used correctly and are defined in tailwind.config.ts. No arbitrary hex values. No picsum.photos references anywhere in the modified code.

issues.description: ConcertCard passes artistImageUrl directly to Next.js Image component with no fallback for empty strings. Seed data currently has empty artistImageUrl values for all concert events. On a fresh database seed, this would cause a Next.js Image runtime error. However, this is a pre-existing issue — the prior version of ConcertCard also used <Image src={artistImageUrl}> without guards. The reviewed commit only changed the layout (from small 24x24 thumbnail to full-width 160px hero), not the image handling logic. A conditional render or fallback placeholder would be advisable.

Basketball decorative elements use absolute positioning with low opacity (opacity-10). While visually fine, the absolute-positioned emojis could potentially overlap interactive content on very small screens. Minimal real-world impact since they're purely decorative.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used standard Tailwind color classes (orange-50, red-50, orange-500, red-500, orange-600, red-600) in BasketballCard, which are NOT defined in the project's tailwind.config.ts custom tokens. AGENTS.md says to use semantic token classes (text-primary, bg-accent, etc.), but doesn't address using default Tailwind palette colors alongside custom tokens. The basketball orange/red palette has no semantic equivalent. This pattern of mixing custom design tokens with default Tailwind colors should be clarified in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: components/basketball/BasketballCard.tsx uses from-orange-50, to-red-50, from-orange-500, to-red-500, border-orange-500, bg-orange-500, from-orange-600, to-red-600 — none of which are custom tokens. tailwind.config.ts only defines primary, accent, success, warning, error, surface, muted color tokens.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ConcertCard uses black/80 and black/40 opacity variants in gradient overlay classes (from-black/80 via-black/40 to-transparent). These are standard Tailwind opacity modifiers, not arbitrary hex values, so they don't violate the AGENTS.md rule. But this pattern of using opacity modifiers on named colors for overlays isn't explicitly blessed in AGENTS.md either.

Empty artistImageUrl in seed data (prisma/seed.ts) causes a latent runtime error with Next.js Image component in ConcertCard. The precondition feature 'real-product-images-and-seed' replaced i.pravatar.cc URLs with empty strings for concert events, creating an incompatibility with any component that uses Next.js <Image>. This knowledge gap could cause failures for future workers who re-seed the database.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: prisma/seed.ts lines 256, 270, 284, 298 all have artistImageUrl: ''. ConcertCard.tsx line 47 passes this directly to <Image src={artistImageUrl}>. git show 7913f48 confirms the precondition feature replaced working URLs with empty strings.

summary: Feature passes review. All 5 validation assertions (VAL-IMG-002 through VAL-IMG-005 plus VAL-ADD-004) are satisfied by the implementation. Home banner shows FIFA World Cup 2026 themed content via gradient-based BannerCarousel. Football cards display 5xl emoji flags in a centered VS layout. Basketball cards have orange-red gradient theming with decorative basketball elements. Concert cards show full-width artist images with dark gradient overlays. Category page banners all use sport-specific gradients and icons. The only non-blocking issue is a pre-existing lack of fallback handling for empty artistImageUrl in ConcertCard, which would cause runtime errors on a fresh database seed. Code quality is good — uses design tokens appropriately, no arbitrary hex values, proper TypeScript typing, and test updated correctly.
